sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Withdraw ₳1,161,000 for zkFold ZK Rollup administered by Intersect

Deliverables: This treasury withdrawal funds zkFold ZK Rollup which will provide the following services: ZK rollups address the scalability problem., Hundreds of transactions can be submitted as a single batch, reducing the load on the L1., Cardano can achieve higher throughput and lower transaction costs., zkFold offers to build a general-purpose zero knowledge rollup solution for Cardano.
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, high materiality): This treasury withdrawal funds zkFold ZK Rollup which will provide the following services: ZK rollups address the scalability problem.
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, low materiality): Hundreds of transactions can be submitted as a single batch, reducing the load on the L1.
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, high materiality): Cardano can achieve higher throughput and lower transaction costs.
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, low materiality): zkFold offers to build a general-purpose zero knowledge rollup solution for Cardano.
Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, low materiality): This proposal aims to solve the following problem: Scalability is a challenge for Cardano.

Claim (economic, independently_verifiable, low materiality): As it grows, more dApps and users join, the demand for processing capacity increases, leading to congestion, slower transaction times, and higher fees.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "This treasury withdrawal funds zkFold ZK Rollup which will provide the following services: ZK rollups address the scalability problem." and clears the evidence gates.
Strongest NO: weak controls, unclear delivery, or governance precedent could outweigh the benefit of TreasuryWithdrawals.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
